title: RFE: podman image scp
body:
Podman has so much ssh stuff in it these days, why not scp?
```console
$ podman image scp myimage:latest otherhost::
$ podman image scp user@otherhost::registry/image:tag .
```
Would basically run `podman image save | ssh host podman image load`. This is something I find myself running pretty often between my home desktop and laptop.

Double colon is a suggestion to deal with the ambiguity between *host:image* and *image:tag*. There may be better ideas.

comments.body: neat idea @edsantiago .. could also use the system connection to do this.

Both great ideas!

A `podman --remote save $image` would work already.

> Both great ideas!
> 
> A `podman --remote save $image` would work already.

Brain fart on my end... `podman --remote load $path` would work.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Would it be best to do this through a remote socket?  IE Have podman use the local cache to save and then use the remote socket to load.
Might be something for an intern.
